column1: # Tab-delimited: checkid<TAB>object<TAB>field<TAB>value   (# comments + blank lines ignored)
# One "check" = one authorization object + the field/value set that must ALL be
# satisfied by a SINGLE authorization instance (exactly one AUTHORITY-CHECK). Rows that
# share a checkid form one group; put each field of the same failed check on its own row.
# Leave value empty for a presence-only check ("does the user have this object at all").
# This is what an SU53 screen decodes to: one checkid per SU53 record, one row per field.
# The header row below (checkid/object/field/value) is optional and skipped if present.
# --- e.g. "cannot maintain the user" -> S_USER_GRP group (two fields, one instance) ---
# --- e.g. "cannot post in company code 1000" -> org-level value ---
